entries.title: Washington State Adopts New Tax on Incomes Over $1 Million
President Trump has a lot on his plate this week. Read more in today’s WSJ Politics Newsletter:
President Trump is weighing a military operation to extract nearly 1,000 pounds of uranium from Iran, a complex mission that would likely put U.S. forces inside the country for days or longer
Iran warns the U.S. after a unit of U.S. Marines arrives in the Middle East, and the stock market shows signs of resilience despite the war. Read more in today’s What’s News newsletter:
Hundreds of thousands of demonstrators are taking to the streets across the nation for a day of “No Kings” rallies Saturday in opposition to President Trump

Investigators searched the office of a FEMA contractor as they investigate a network of aides put in place by former DHS Secretary Kristi Noem and adviser Corey Lewandowski, according to people familiar with the matter
President Trump’s tax cuts are delivering bigger refunds and smaller tax bills to high-income, Democratic-leaning regions that didn’t vote for him
President Trump signed an executive order to pay TSA workers, bypassing a gridlocked Congress after the latest proposal to fund the broader Department of Homeland Security ran aground Friday
California Gov. Gavin Newsom signed an executive order banning gubernatorial appointees from using nonpublic information to profit on prediction markets
The annual Conservative Political Action Conference has laid bare a rift on the right. Some are asking: “What happened to America First?”
Hartsfield-Jackson Atlanta International Airport is a chokepoint in the federal funding standoff in Congress, and officials warned travelers not to expect lines to ease over the weekend
Missouri Republican Rep. Sam Graves, chairman of the House Committee on Transportation, is pulling his plans to seek re-election
President Trump has told associates in recent days that he wants to avoid a protracted war in Iran and that he hopes to bring the conflict to an end in the coming weeks
Democrat Emily Gregory beat a Trump-backed Republican in a special election for the Florida state house district that includes President Trump’s Mar-a-Lago estate
The Supreme Court seems inclined to block states from counting mailed ballots that are postmarked by Election Day but arrive a few days afterward
President Trump has demanded that the GOP-controlled Senate vote to end most mail-in voting. Here’s why some Republicans are pushing back
If President Trump gives the go-ahead, the U.S. could soon have over 17,000 troops on Iran’s doorstep, but any operation to seize an island or territory would be considered complex and dangerous
Billions of dollars of sophisticated military equipment has been lost or significantly damaged since the U.S. and Israel began striking thousands of targets across Iran more than three weeks ago
The Defense Department introduced new restrictions on press access on Monday, days after a federal judge ruled that key elements of the limits it introduced last year were unconstitutional
Anduril Industries and Palantir Technologies are among firms developing the software to run the $185 billion Golden Dome antimissile shield
The U.S. war with Iran has triggered the broadest and fastest wave of attacks against U.S. embassies and consulates in the post-9/11 era, current and former officials said
